1.description: CMBS deal flow. This figure displays the flow of commercial mortgages into CMBS from originators to lead managers (underwriters) for CMBS deals issued from 2013 to 2019. Flows are shown for the top 15 deal managers by deal volume and the top 50 originators. The size of the nodes is proportional to total volume originated for originator or total deal volume managed for underwriters, with the scale of the nodes being smaller for underwriters than for originators, so that each can be seen. The size of the flow between an originator and manager is proportional to the thickness of the edge. Affiliated flows, defined as a flow in which an originator sells a mortgage to a pool managed by an affiliate, are given by maroon flows while nonaffiliated capital loan flow is shown in light green. The breakdown by property type is given by the pie charts within each node. The network is directed, so flows are always from an originator to a lead manager. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Overstatement by originator. This figure displays income overstatement for large originators in the sample. Income overstatement is defined as(Underwritten NOI − Realized NOI)/Underwritten NOI. A value greater than zero indicates that underwritten NOI exceeded realized NOI in the CMBS deal year. The proportion of loans for which income was overstated by more than 5% is indicated by diamonds. The bars denote 95% confidence intervals for this proportion. Red denotes originators who manage nonagency CMBS, and blue denotes originators who do not manage CMBS. The black line denotes the overall sample average. Originators generating more than 1% of the loans in the sample (>400 loans) are included. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Is income overstatement persistent across originators?This figure examines the persistence of income overstatement by originators. Hunt is excluded due to having insufficient loans in the early (2013 to 2015) period. The persistence of income overstatement is demonstrated in Panel A. In Panel B, we control for loan- and property-level characteristics, along with additional unobserved variation by estimating the following regression separately for the early and late subperiods:HighOveri=α+θXi+∑k=1KγkOriginatork+FixedEffects+εi$HighOver_i=\alpha +\theta X_i +\sum _{k=1} ^K \gamma _k Originator_k+ Fixed {\textit {Effects}}+\epsilon _i$, (2) whereXi$X_i$is the vector of loan controls including LTV, DSCR, interest rate, (log) size, building age, occupancy, property type, and interest rate type controls, and fixed effects includeMSA×Year,MSA×Property Type, andProperty Type×Class×Quarter. We plot the estimated originator fixed effects from equation (2) in Panel B. Red circles denote originators who manage nonagency CMBS deals, while blue circles denote originators who do not manage CMBS deals. Circle size corresponds to origination volume. The black line is a 45° line and the correlation is presented at the bottom of each graph. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: Income overstatement and Kroll predictions. This figure displays the relationship between income overstatement and predicted income overstatement by Kroll Bond Rating Agency.NOI Overstatementis defined as(Underwritten NOI − Realized NOI)/Underwritten NOI. A value greater than zero indicates that underwritten NOI exceeded realized NOI.Kroll Haircutis defined(Underwritten Cash Flow − Kroll Cash Flow)/Underwritten Cash Flow. The data are sorted from low to high into deciles based on the Kroll haircut measure in Panel A and displayed by originator in Panel B. In Panel B, red circles denote originators who manage nonagency CMBS deals and blue circles denote originators who do not manage CMBS deals. Circle size corresponds to origination volume. The green line is a linear fit of the averages and the correlation is presented at the bottom. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: Example of past income inflation. This exhibit demonstrates an example of income inflation. The property income as reported in the Investor Reporting Package from the original securitization (WFRBS 2012-C7) is denoted by the blue line. The blue line shows the 2015 and 2016 annualized NOI as reported in the original securitization. The dark green line denotes the historical income for 2015 and 2016 that was reported in the new securitization (WFCM 2017-C38). The dark green X denotes the underwritten income. Finally, the light green line shows the realized income as reported in the new securitization for years 2017 and 2018. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

6.description: NOI overstatement and past inflation. This figure displays the relationship between previous-year NOI inflation and NOI overstatement. The previous-year NOI inflation is calculated as the difference between the income for a property reported in the new CMBS and that reported in the original CMBS, in the same financial year. Because prospectuses typically report income for multiple years, we measure average inflation over the two most recent years. In Panel A, we demonstrate the proportion of loans for each originator for which income in the new deal is inflated by at least 5% relative to what was reported in the previous deal (orange bars) and the proportion of deals with reported income that is at least 5% less than previously reported (light blue bars). In Panel B, we plot the proportion of loans that have historical NOI inflated by at least 5% in the new deal on thex-axis and the proportion of loans with underwritten income overstatement of at least 5% on they-axis. The black line is a linear fit of the data and the correlation is presented in the bottom left corner. In Panel C, we present a binscatter of the probability of income overstatement on the percent by which historical NOI was inflated in new deal documents. Controls for underwritten LTV, DSCR, (log) size, (log) building age, occupancy, property type, interest rate type (fixed or adjustable), and amortization type and fixed effects forMSA×Year,MSA×Property Type, andProperty Type×Class×Quarterare included. The fitted values are given by the red lines. To reduce the impact of outliers in the figure, NOI inflation is winsorized at the 1% tails. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

7.description: Is income overstatement priced by originators?This figure explores the relationship between income overstatement and loan interest rates. We estimate the regression:InterestRatei=α+βIncomeOverstatementi+θXi+FixedEffects+εi$InterestRate_i=\alpha +\beta IncomeOverstatement_i + \theta X_i + Fixed {\textit {Effects}} + \epsilon _i$, and in Panel A we plot the slope onIncomeOverstatementi$IncomeOverstatement_i$, which is a continuous variable defined as the percent by which underwritten income is overstated for loani. The vector of controlsXi$X_i$includes UW LTV, DSCR, (log) size, (log) building age, occupancy, property type, interest rate type (fixed or adjustable), and amortization type. Fixed effects includeMSA×Year,MSA×Property Type, andProperty Type×Class×Quarter. Loans are assigned to 30 bins based on income overstatement. In Panel B, in addition to controls and fixed effects described above, interest rate and overstatement are further residualized based on originator fixed effects. To reduce the impact of extreme outliers, we winsorize income overstatement at the 1% tails. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

8.description: Income overstatement and loan distress by geographic area. This figure displays the proportion of distressed loans in each (three-digit) ZIP code. Loans are considered distressed if they are on a servicer's Watchlist or are nonperforming. Troubled loans are placed on a servicer's Watchlist for failing to meet criteria that often precede default including various forms of delinquency or failure to meet sufficient debt-service coverage ratios. Nonperforming means that a loan has missed a payment, is in the process of foreclosure, is real estate owned, or has been placed into special servicing. Originators are sorted into terciles based on the ex antePastOverkt$PastOver_{kt}$measure, which is defined as the proportion of loans by originatorkthat have income overstated by more than 5% in all years prior to the deal yeart. The proportion of distressed loans is displayed separately for originators in each tercile type. To be included on the map, we require that there be at least four loans from the given type of originator within each ZIP. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

9.description: Distress and past originator overstatement. This figure investigates the relationship between originator overstatement practices. Originators are sorted into terciles based on their past overstatement practices as measured by the ex antePastOverkt$PastOver_{kt}$measure, which is defined as the proportion of loans by originatorkthat overstate income by more than 5% in all years prior to the deal yeart. The lines display the proportion of loans at each point in time that are distressed. Loans are considered distressed if they have been placed on a servicer's Watchlist or are nonperforming. The dashed lines denote 95% confidence intervals. Data for loan performance are from May 2016 to April 2021. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

10.description: Income overstatement and loan distress. This figure displays the relationship between income overstatement and the probability of distress by originator. Panel A shows the proportion of loans by each originator with income overstatement greater than 5% and the probability of a loan being distressed from April 2020 to April 2021. In Panel B, we first estimate a regression of the formHighOveri=α+θXi+∑k=1KγkOriginatork+FixedEffects+εi$HighOver_i=\alpha +\theta X_i +\sum _{k=1} ^K \gamma _k Originator_k+Fixed {\textit {Effects}}+\epsilon _i$,   (2) whereHighOveri$HighOver_i$is a dummy variable equal to one if the underwritten income on loaniis overstated by more than 5%,Xi$X_i$is a vector of loan controls including LTV, DSCR, interest rate, (log) size, building age, occupancy, property type, and interest rate type, and fixed effects includeMSA×Year,MSA×Property Type, andProperty Type×Class×Quarter. We next estimate a regression of the same specification, except the outcome variable is aDistress$Distress$dummy variable equal to one if the loan has been placed on a servicer's Watchlist or is nonperforming during April 2020 to April 2021. We plot the estimated originator fixed effects from these two specifications in Panel B. Red circles denote originators who manage nonagency CMBS deals and blue circles denote other originators. The green line fits a linear regression between proportion distressed loans and proportion high-income overstatement loans by originator in Panel A and between estimated originator fixed effects in Panel B. The correlation coefficients are reported at the bottom of the graph. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
